 78→* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
 79→* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
 80→
 81→## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 82→
 83→### Added
 84→
 85→### Removed
 86→
 87→### Fixed
 88→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 89→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 90→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 91→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
 92→* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
 93→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
 94→* Fix indentation of multiline parameter list in function literal `indent` ([#1976](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1976))
 95→* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
 96→* Extract rule `no-single-line-block-comment` from `comment-wrapping` rule. The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))

  70→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_CONSTRAINT
  71→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST
  72→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST
  73→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE
  74→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.USER_TYPE
  75→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT
  76→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST
  77→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER
  78→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
  79→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHEN
  80→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHEN_ENTRY
  81→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHERE_KEYWORD
  82→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHILE
  83→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  84→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig

  86→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.TAB

  88→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  89→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
  90→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
  91→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue
  92→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official
  93→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  94→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  95→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY

  99→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment

 101→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 102→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 103→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline
 104→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 105→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 106→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeSibling
 107→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 108→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 109→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.parent
 110→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 111→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeSibling
 112→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf

 115→import mu.KotlinLogging
 116→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 117→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiComment
 118→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 119→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtStringTemplateExpression

 124→
 125→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 126→
 127→public class IndentationRule :
 128→    StandardRule(
 129→        id = "indent",
 130→        visitorModifiers =
 131→            setOf(
 132→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 133→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 134→                    ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
 135→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 136→                ),
 137→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 138→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 139→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 140→                ),
 141→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 142→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 143→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 144→                ),
 145→            ),
 146→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 147→            setOf(
 148→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 149→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 150→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 151→            ),
 152→    ) {
 153→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 154→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 155→
 156→    private var line = 1
 157→
 158→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 159→
 160→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 161→
 162→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 163→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 164→        indentConfig =
 165→            IndentConfig(
 166→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 167→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 168→            )
 169→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 170→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 171→        }
 172→    }
 173→
 174→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 175→        node: ASTNode,
 176→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 177→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 178→    ) {
 179→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 180→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 181→            node
 182→                .nextLeaf()
 183→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 184→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 185→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 186→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 187→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 188→                    }
 189→                }
 190→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 191→        }
 192→
 193→        when {
 194→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 195→                line++
 196→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 197→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 198→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 199→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 200→                    )
 201→                }
 202→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 203→            }
 204→
 205→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 206→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 207→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 208→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
 209→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 210→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 211→                startIndentContext(
 212→                    fromAstNode = node,
 213→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 214→                )
 215→
 216→            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 217→                visitValueArgument(node)
 218→
 219→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 220→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 221→
 222→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 223→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 224→                startIndentContext(node)
 225→
 226→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 227→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 228→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 229→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 230→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 231→                startIndentContext(node)
 232→
 233→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 234→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 235→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 236→                startIndentContext(
 237→                    fromAstNode = node,
 238→                    childIndent = "",
 239→                )
 240→
 241→            node.elementType == IF ->
 242→                visitIf(node)
 243→
 244→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 245→                visitLbrace(node)
 246→
 247→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 248→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 249→                startIndentContext(
 250→                    fromAstNode = node,
 251→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 252→                )
 253→
 254→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 255→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 256→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 257→
 258→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 259→                visitValueParameter(node)
 260→
 261→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 262→                visitFun(node)
 263→
 264→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 265→                visitClass(node)
 266→
 267→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 268→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 269→
 270→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 271→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 272→
 273→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 274→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 275→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 276→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 277→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 278→                ) {
 279→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 280→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 281→                    //    .bar[0] {
 282→                    //        "foobar"
 283→                    //    }
 284→                    startIndentContext(
 285→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 286→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 287→                    )
 288→                } else if (node.prevCodeSibling().isElvisOperator()) {
 289→                    startIndentContext(node)
 290→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 291→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 292→                } else {
 293→                    startIndentContext(node)
 294→                }
 295→            }
 296→
 297→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 298→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 299→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 300→
 301→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 302→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 303→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 304→
 305→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 306→                visitWhen(node)
 307→
 308→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 309→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 310→
 311→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 312→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 313→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 314→
 315→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 316→                visitKdoc(node)
 317→
 318→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 319→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 320→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 321→
 322→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 323→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 324→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 325→
 326→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 327→                visitLBracket(node)
 328→
 329→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 330→                visitNullableType(node)
 331→
 332→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 333→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 334→
 335→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 336→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 337→
 338→            else -> {
 339→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 340→            }
 341→        }
 342→    }
 343→
 344→    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 345→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 346→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 347→            //     val foo = foo(
 348→            //         parameterName =
 349→            //             "The quick brown fox "
 350→            //                .plus("jumps ")
 351→            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 352→            //     )
 353→            startIndentContext(
 354→                fromAstNode = node,
 355→                lastChildIndent = "",
 356→            )
 357→        }
 358→    }
 359→
 360→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 361→        node
 362→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 363→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 364→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 365→                val nextToAstNode =
 366→                    startIndentContext(
 367→                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 368→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 369→
 370→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 371→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 372→                    startIndentContext(
 373→                        fromAstNode = node,
 374→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 375→                        childIndent = "",
 376→                    )
 377→                }
 378→            }
 379→    }
 380→
 381→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 382→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 383→        node
 384→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 385→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 386→                nextToAstNode =
 387→                    startIndentContext(
 388→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 389→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 390→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 391→            }
 392→
 393→        node
 394→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 395→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 396→            ?.nextLeaf()
 397→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 398→                nextToAstNode =
 399→                    startIndentContext(
 400→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 401→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 402→                        childIndent = "",
 403→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 404→            }
 405→        node
 406→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 407→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 408→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 409→                nextToAstNode =
 410→                    startIndentContext(
 411→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 412→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 413→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 414→            }
 415→        startIndentContext(
 416→            fromAstNode = node,
 417→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 418→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 419→        )
 420→    }
 421→
 422→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 423→        // Outer indent context
 424→        val rbrace =
 425→            requireNotNull(
 426→                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 427→            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 428→        startIndentContext(
 429→            fromAstNode = node,
 430→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 431→            firstChildIndent = "",
 432→            lastChildIndent = "",
 433→        )
 434→
 435→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 436→        node
 437→            .treeParent
 438→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 439→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 440→            ?.let { arrow ->
 441→                startIndentContext(
 442→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 443→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 444→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 445→                )
 446→                startIndentContext(
 447→                    fromAstNode = node,
 448→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 449→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 450→                )
 451→            }
 452→    }
 453→
 454→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 455→        if (isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 456→            // val fieldExample =
 457→            //      LongNameClass {
 458→            //              paramA,
 459→            //              paramB,
 460→            //              paramC ->
 461→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 462→            //      }
 463→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 464→        } else {
 465→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 466→            // val fieldExample =
 467→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 468→            //                     paramB,
 469→            //                     paramC ->
 470→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 471→            //     }
 472→            // val fieldExample =
 473→            //     someFunction(
 474→            //         1,
 475→            //         2,
 476→            //     ) { paramA,
 477→            //         paramB,
 478→            //         paramC ->
 479→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 480→            //     }
 481→            this
 482→                .takeIf { it.isPartOf(CALL_EXPRESSION) }
 483→                ?.treeParent
 484→                ?.leaves(false)
 485→                ?.takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
 486→                ?.sumOf { it.textLength }
 487→                ?.plus(2) // need to add spaces to compensate for "{ "
 488→                ?.let { length -> " ".repeat(length) }
 489→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 490→        }
 491→
 492→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 493→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 494→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 495→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 496→
 497→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 498→        startIndentContext(
 499→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 500→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 501→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 502→            childIndent = "",
 503→        )
 504→    }
 505→
 506→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 507→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 508→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 509→        node
 510→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 511→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 512→                nextToAstNode =
 513→                    startIndentContext(
 514→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 515→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 516→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 517→            }
 518→
 519→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 520→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 521→            //     fun process(
 522→            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 523→            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 524→            //     ): List<Output>
 525→            node
 526→                .findChildByType(COLON)
 527→                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 528→                    nextToAstNode =
 529→                        startIndentContext(
 530→                            fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 531→                            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 532→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 533→                }
 534→        }
 535→
 536→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 537→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 538→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 539→            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 540→            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 541→        ) {
 542→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 543→                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 544→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 545→            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 546→        } else {
 547→            startIndentContext(
 548→                fromAstNode = node,
 549→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 550→                childIndent = "",
 551→            )
 552→        }
 553→    }
 554→
 555→    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 556→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 557→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 558→        val eqOrBlock =
 559→            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 560→                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 561→        eqOrBlock?.let {
 562→            nextToAstNode =
 563→                startIndentContext(
 564→                    fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 565→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 566→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 567→        }
 568→
 569→        node
 570→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 571→            ?.let { typeReference ->
 572→                nextToAstNode =
 573→                    startIndentContext(
 574→                        fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 575→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 576→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 577→            }
 578→
 579→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 580→        startIndentContext(
 581→            fromAstNode = node,
 582→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 583→            childIndent = "",
 584→        )
 585→    }
 586→
 587→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 588→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 589→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 590→        node
 591→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 592→            ?.let { where ->
 593→                val typeConstraintList =
 594→                    requireNotNull(
 595→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 596→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 597→                require(
 598→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 599→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 600→                nextToAstNode =
 601→                    startIndentContext(
 602→                        fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 603→                        toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 604→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 605→            }
 606→
 607→        val primaryConstructor = node.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 608→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && primaryConstructor != null) {
 609→            // Indent both constructor and super type list
 610→            nextToAstNode =
 611→                startIndentContext(
 612→                    fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 613→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 614→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 615→        } else {
 616→            node
 617→                .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 618→                ?.let { superTypeList ->
 619→                    nextToAstNode =
 620→                        startIndentContext(
 621→                            fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 622→                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 623→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 624→                }
 625→        }
 626→
 627→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 628→        startIndentContext(
 629→            fromAstNode = node,
 630→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 631→            childIndent = "",
 632→        )
 633→    }
 634→
 635→    private fun visitObjectDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 636→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 637→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 638→        node
 639→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 640→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 641→                nextToAstNode =
 642→                    startIndentContext(
 643→                        fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 644→                        toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 645→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 646→            }
 647→
 648→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 649→        startIndentContext(
 650→            fromAstNode = node,
 651→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 652→            childIndent = "",
 653→        )
 654→    }
 655→
 656→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 657→        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 658→            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 659→            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 660→            val conditionIndentContext =
 661→                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 662→            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 663→            startIndentContext(
 664→                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 665→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 666→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 667→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 668→            )
 669→            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 670→        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 671→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 672→        ) {
 673→            startIndentContext(node)
 674→        }
 675→    }

 677→    private fun visitIdentifierInProperty(node: ASTNode) {
 678→        startIndentContext(
 679→            fromAstNode = node,

 681→        )

 684→    private fun visitWhen(node: ASTNode) {
 685→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 686→        node
 687→            .findChildByType(LPAR)

 690→                    startIndentContext(
 691→                        fromAstNode = lpar,
 692→                        toAstNode = node.findChildByType(RPAR)!!,
 693→                        lastChildIndent = "",
 694→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 695→            }

 697→            fromAstNode = node,
 698→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,

 715→                    firstChildIndent = outerIndent,

 766→    private fun visitConditionalLoop(node: ASTNode) {

 877→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 878→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 879→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 880→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 881→                modifierList
 882→                    .children()
 883→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 884→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 885→            }
 886→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 887→            ?: this
 888→    }

 890→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 891→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 892→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 893→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 894→        ) {
 895→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 896→        }
 897→        return fromAstNode!!
 898→    }

 900→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =
 901→        node
 902→            .parents()
 903→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 904→            .lastOrNull()
 905→            ?.elementType == CONDITION

 907→    private fun currentIndent() =
 908→        indentContextStack
 909→            .peekLast()
 910→            .indent()
 911→
 912→    private fun startIndentContext(
 913→        fromAstNode: ASTNode,
 914→        toAstNode: ASTNode = fromAstNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 915→        nodeIndent: String = currentIndent(),
 916→        childIndent: String = indentConfig.indent,
 917→        firstChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 918→        lastChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 919→    ): IndentContext =
 920→        IndentContext(
 921→            fromASTNode = fromAstNode,
 922→            toASTNode = toAstNode,
 923→            nodeIndent = nodeIndent,
 924→            firstChildIndent = firstChildIndent,
 925→            childIndent = childIndent,
 926→            lastChildIndent = lastChildIndent,
 927→        ).also { newIndentContext ->
 928→            LOGGER.trace {
 929→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.nodeIndent)
 930→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.childIndent)
 931→                "Create new indent context (same as parent) with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 932→                    "${fromAstNode.elementType}: ${newIndentContext.nodes}"
 933→            }
 934→            indentContextStack.addLast(newIndentContext)
 935→        }
 936→
 937→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
 938→        node: ASTNode,
 939→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 940→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 941→    ) {
 942→        while (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.toASTNode == node) {
 943→            LOGGER.trace {
 944→                val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 945→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 946→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 947→                "Remove indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for ${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: " +
 948→                    "${indentContext.nodes}"
 949→            }
 950→            indentContextStack
 951→                .removeLast()
 952→                .also {
 953→                    LOGGER.trace {
 954→                        val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 955→                        val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 956→                        val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 957→                        "Last indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 958→                            "${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: ${indentContext.nodes}"
 959→                    }
 960→                }
 961→        }
 962→    }
 963→
 964→    override fun afterLastNode() {
 965→        require(indentContextStack.isEmpty()) {
 966→            indentContextStack
 967→                .joinToString(prefix = "Stack should be empty:\n\t", separator = "\n\t") { it.toString() }
 968→        }
 969→    }
 970→
 971→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 972→        node: ASTNode,
 973→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 974→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 975→    ) {
 976→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 977→            return
 978→        }
 979→
 980→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 981→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 982→        val text = node.text
 983→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 984→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 985→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 986→                emit(
 987→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 988→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 989→                    true,
 990→                )
 991→            } else {
 992→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 993→                // been emitted.
 994→            }
 995→            LOGGER.trace {
 996→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 997→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 998→            }
 999→            if (autoCorrect) {
1000→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1001→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
1002→                )
1003→            }
1004→        } else {
1005→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1006→        }
1007→    }
1008→
1009→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
1010→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1011→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
1012→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1013→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
1014→        }
1015→
1016→        nextLeaf
1017→            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
1018→            ?.let { comment ->
1019→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
1020→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1021→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
1022→                }
1023→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
1024→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
1025→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
1026→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1027→                    return true
1028→                }
1029→            }
1030→        return false
1031→    }
1032→
1033→    private fun ASTNode?.isStartOfRawStringLiteral(): Boolean = this != null && this.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE && this.text == "\"\"\""
1034→
1035→    private fun ASTNode.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded() =
1036→        LOGGER.trace { "No indentation change required for $elementType: ${textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
1037→
1038→    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
1039→        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
1040→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1041→        val adjustedChildIndent =
1042→            when {
1043→                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
1044→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1045→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1046→
1047→                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1048→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1049→                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1050→
1051→                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
1052→            }
1053→        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
1054→    }
1055→
1056→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1057→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1058→        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1059→            SPACE -> {
1060→                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1061→                    emit(
1062→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1063→                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1064→                        true,
1065→                    )
1066→                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1067→                } else {
1068→                    nodeIndent
1069→                }
1070→            }
1071→
1072→            TAB -> {
1073→                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1074→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1075→                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1076→                    emit(
1077→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1078→                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1079→                        true,
1080→                    )
1081→                    indentConfig
1082→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1083→                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1084→                } else {
1085→                    nodeIndent
1086→                }
1087→            }
1088→        }
1089→    }
1090→
1091→    private fun visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(
1092→        node: ASTNode,
1093→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1094→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1095→    ) {
1096→        if (!this::stringTemplateIndenter.isInitialized) {
1097→            stringTemplateIndenter = StringTemplateIndenter(codeStyle, indentConfig)
1098→        }
1099→        stringTemplateIndenter.visitClosingQuotes(currentIndent(), node.treeParent, autoCorrect, emit)
1100→    }
1101→
1102→    private fun ASTNode?.isElvisOperator() =
1103→        this != null &&
1104→            elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE &&
1105→            firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
1106→
1107→    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1108→        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1109→        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1110→            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1111→                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1112→                    // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1113→                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1114→                    KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1115→                }
1116→
1117→                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1118→                    // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1119→                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1120→                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1121→                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1122→                }
1123→
1124→                else -> ""
1125→            }
1126→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1127→        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1128→            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1129→        } else {
1130→            ""
1131→        }
1132→    }
1133→
1134→    private fun startNoIndentZone(node: ASTNode) =
1135→        IndentContext(
1136→            fromASTNode = node,
1137→            nodeIndent = "",
1138→            firstChildIndent = "",
1139→            childIndent = "",
1140→            lastChildIndent = "",
1141→            activated = true,
1142→        )
1143→
1144→    private companion object {
1145→        const val KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT = " "
1146→        const val TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT = "      " // Length of keyword "where" plus separating space
1147→        val CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION = setOf(DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION, SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION)
1148→    }
1149→
1150→    private data class IndentContext(
1151→        /**
1152→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1153→         */
1154→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1155→        /**
1156→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1157→         * on which the indent context starts.
1158→         */
1159→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1160→        /**
1161→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1162→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1163→         */
1164→        val nodeIndent: String,
1165→        /**
1166→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1167→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1168→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1169→         */
1170→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1171→        /**
1172→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1173→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1174→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1175→         */
1176→        val childIndent: String,
1177→        /**
1178→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1179→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1180→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1181→         */
1182→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1183→        /**
1184→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1185→         */
1186→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1187→    ) {
1188→        val nodes: String
1189→            get() =
1190→                fromASTNode
1191→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1192→                    ?.leaves()
1193→                    .orEmpty()
1194→                    .takeWhile {
1195→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1196→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1197→                    }
1198→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1199→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1200→
1201→        fun indent() =
1202→            if (activated) {
1203→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1204→            } else {
1205→                nodeIndent
1206→            }
1207→
1208→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1209→    }
1210→}
1211→
1212→private fun ASTNode.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline() = text.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1213→
1214→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1215→    val (prefix, suffix) =
1216→        if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1217→            Pair("[", "]")
1218→        } else {
1219→            Pair("", "")
1220→        }
1221→    return prefix
1222→        .plus(
1223→            this
1224→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1225→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1226→        ).plus(suffix)
1227→}
1228→
1229→private class StringTemplateIndenter(
1230→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
1231→    private val indentConfig: IndentConfig,
1232→) {
1233→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1234→        expectedIndent: String,
1235→        node: ASTNode,
1236→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1237→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1238→    ) {
1239→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1240→        node
1241→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1242→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1243→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1244→            ?.let { _ ->
1245→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1246→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1247→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1248→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1249→                    emit(
1250→                        node.startOffset,
1251→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1252→                        false,
1253→                    )
1254→                    return
1255→                }
1256→
1257→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1258→                val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
1259→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1260→                    if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock()) {
1261→                        // Allow:
1262→                        //   fun foo(): String {
1263→                        //       return """
1264→                        //           some text
1265→                        //           """.trimIndent
1266→                        //   }
1267→                        node
1268→                            .indent(false)

1432→     * consists of the indentation and the second part contains the remainder. Note that the second part still can start
1433→     * with whitespace characters in case the original strings starts with more white space characters than the requested
1434→     * split index.

1436→    private fun String.splitIndentAt(index: Int): Pair<String, String> {

1439→            indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let {
1440→                if (it == -1) {
1441→                    this.length
1442→                } else {
1443→                    it
1444→                }
1445→            }

1448→            first = this.take(safeIndex),
1449→            second = this.substring(safeIndex),
1450→        )
1451→    }

1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→### Added
 8→
 9→### Removed
10→
11→### Fixed
12→
13→### Changed
14→
15→* Update dependency gradle to v8.2 ([#2105](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2105))
16→
17→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
18→
19→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
20→
21→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
22→
23→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
24→
25→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
26→
27→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
28→
29→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
30→

587→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
588→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
589→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
590→        node
591→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
592→            ?.let { where ->
593→                val typeConstraintList =
594→                    requireNotNull(
595→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
596→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
597→                require(
598→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
599→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
600→                nextToAstNode =
601→                    startIndentContext(
602→                        fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
603→                        toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
604→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
605→            }
606→
607→        val primaryConstructor = node.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
608→        val superTypeList = node.findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
609→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && primaryConstructor != null) {
610→            // Indent constructor and super type list, but not the class body
611→            val toNode = superTypeList?.lastChildLeafOrSelf() ?: primaryConstructor.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
612→            nextToAstNode =
613→                startIndentContext(
614→                    fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
615→                    toAstNode = toNode,
616→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
617→        } else if (superTypeList != null) {
618→            nextToAstNode =
619→                startIndentContext(
620→                    fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
621→                    toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
622→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
623→        }
624→
625→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
626→        startIndentContext(
627→            fromAstNode = node,
628→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
629→            childIndent = "",
630→        )
631→    }
